codeReview.summary: The commit does split the three target panel files into focused submodules that all stay under 200 lines, and the tabs/workflows extractions are behavior-equivalent on inspection. However, the history split is not fully behavior-preserving: `persistHistory` no longer saves `contextTranscript`, so sessions saved after this refactor lose the richer raw context that `loadSession` and workflow generation depend on. The history list also dropped its transcript-length fallback for older stored sessions, which can surface incorrect `0 messages` counts.

issues.description: The refactor stopped persisting `contextTranscript` when saving session history. Before the split, `panel-history.ts` normalized and stored `this.contextHistory` alongside `transcript`; after the split only `messageCount` and `transcript` are written here. `history-session-loader.ts` still looks for `session.contextTranscript` when reloading a session, and `workflows-session-context.ts` consumes `this.contextHistory` for workflow generation, so sessions saved after this commit no longer reload with the full raw context they previously preserved.

The list now renders `const msgCount = session.messageCount || 0;`, dropping the previous fallback to `transcript.length`. Older stored sessions without `messageCount` will show `0 messages` even when a transcript exists, which is a user-visible regression in the history panel.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `refactor-worker` skill's final-validation contract is still easy for workers to overstate in handoff metadata: this session marked `followedProcedure: true` without actually running all required validations.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/skills/refactor-worker/SKILL.md:98,101,130` require `npm run test:integration`, `npm run lint`, and say `followedProcedure: true` is only valid if all validation steps actually ran and passed. Handoff `/Users/sero/.factory/missions/fdd24bf3-fc74-45b3-a646-4179615c1e31/handoffs/2026-03-11T13-43-25-382Z__sidepanel-refactor-panels-remaining__996f7a07-d005-4285-931b-664e7bb60b8e.json` records only `npm run typecheck`, `npm run build`, `npm run test:unit`, `npm run check:repo-standards`, and `wc -l`, yet still sets `skillFeedback.followedProcedure` to true.

summary: Fail: the refactor meets the file-splitting goal, but it regresses history persistence by dropping `contextTranscript`, so saved sessions no longer reload with the full context the pre-refactor implementation preserved. I also noted a non-blocking history-count regression for legacy sessions and a skill-reporting gap around skipped lint/integration validation.
